body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule` **Service directory:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/` **Triggered by:** commit 53334f87b71764f06f53db0495eb500c1ab6c617 (PR #57188) by @azure-sdk (co-authored by @Asifmammadov89) ## What Changed PR #57188 added support for the `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` API operation (introduced in API version `2026-03-01-preview`). This operation enables on-demand flexible VM provisioning with Spot or regular priority, configurable VM size profiles, OS type, and allocation strategy. The PR added scenario tests for this operation but no README documentation or test-backed code snippets. ## Gaps Found - The README **Examples** section only links to a generic management samples page — it has no package-specific code snippets for any of the ComputeSchedule operations - The new `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` operation has no sample code or documentation showing how to use it (required parameters, priority type enum, VM size profiles, allocation strategy, inline network config) - None of the existing sample files in `tests/Samples/` use `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers, so **no samples are wired up** to appear in the README via the snippet injection system - The README already mentions `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` in the intro but does not demonstrate usage with a code example
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` — **Edit** Replace the current minimal Examples section: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples) ``` With an expanded section that includes placeholder blocks for the snippet tool to inject: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples) ### Execute an immediate VM start ```C# Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart ``` ### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex) ```C# Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### Snippet 1 — Execute start **Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` Wrap an existing `VirtualMachinesExecuteStart` test method body with a named region (or add a new dedicated sample method): ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task ExecuteStart_Sample() { #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); var resources = new ExecuteResourceOperationContent( new ResourceOperationDetails( new UserRequestRetryPolicy(RetryCount.One, RetryWindowInMinutes.Thirty), new List { new ResourceId("/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}") }), new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString())); Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteStartAsync( AzureLocation.EastUS, resources); Console.WriteLine($"Operation result: {response.Value.Type}"); #endregion } ``` **README location:** Under the `### Execute an immediate VM start` heading added in Step 1. **Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart` --- #### Snippet 2 — ExecuteCreateFlex **Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` Add a new sample method: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task ExecuteCreateFlex_Sample() { #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); var flexRequest = new ExecuteCreateFlexContent( new CreateFlexDetails( new List { new VmSizeProfile("Standard_D2s_v3") { Rank = 1 }, new VmSizeProfile("Standard_D4s_v3") { Rank = 2 } }, OSType.Windows, AllocationStrategy.Prioritized, new InlineNetworkInterfaceConfiguration( new SubnetConfiguration("/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnet}/subnets/{subnet}"))), new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString())) { PriorityType = PriorityType.Spot }; Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlexAsync( AzureLocation.EastUS, flexRequest); Console.WriteLine($"Created VMs: {response.Value.Results.Count}"); #endregion } ``` **README location:** Under the `### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex)` heading added in Step 1. **Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections. The **Examples** section (gap #1 above) needs to be fleshed out with actual snippet placeholders as described above. All other sections are present but reference to `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` should remain in the intro. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 computeschedule` — check the git diff afterward to confirm README snippet blocks were updated with the sample code 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 computeschedule` — only required if public API surface changed; in this case the API was already added, so verify no new API listing changes are needed 5. `dotnet format sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [ComputeSchedule] Docs: Add code snippets for VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex and wire README examples
